---
title: "Alex Knorre"
lightbox: false
page-layout: article


---

```{=html}
<img src="files/knorre_headshot.jpg"
     alt="Alex Knorre"
     style="float: left;
            width: min(280px, 35%);
            margin: .25em 1.5em 0.25em 0em;
            border-radius: 10px;">
```

I study how we know what we think we know about crime and violence. I work on measurement problems in criminology, especially where official data is missing, manipulated, or systematically biased: crime victims, illegal markets, and criminal justice in settings with politicized administrative data. My work combines new measurement methods, descriptive evidence on hard-to-measure populations and behaviors, and causal evaluation of the natural experiments or policies that affect them. Building reproducible research infrastructure is a core part of how I work, and I release the underlying data and code when possible so other researchers can build on it.

I am an Assistant Professor in the Department of Criminology at [the University of South Florida](https://www.usf.edu/cbcs/criminology/faculty-staff/a-knorre.aspx). I received my Ph.D. in Criminology and A.M. in Statistics from [the University of Pennsylvania](https://crim.sas.upenn.edu), and worked as a Postdoctoral Research Fellow in Computer Science at [Boston College](https://www.bc.edu/content/bc-web/schools/morrissey/departments/computer-science.html). Before joining Ph.D. program, I studied Russian criminal justice as a researcher with the Institute for the Rule of Law, [European University at St. Petersburg](https://eusp.org/en). Recent work appears in *PLoS One*, *Justice Quarterly*, *Journal of Criminal Justice*, *Aggression & Violent Behavior*, and *Criminology & Public Policy*.

My CV is available here: [PDF, 120Kb](./files/Knorre_CV_Feb2026.pdf)

My profiles: [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alexeyknorre/), [Github](https://github.com/alexknorre), [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=mR-1XBIAAAAJ&hl=en).

## Papers

```{=html}

<script>
document.addEventListener('click', function(e) {
  if (e.target.closest('.paper-entry img')) {
    var overlay = document.createElement('div');
    overlay.className = 'img-overlay';
    var img = document.createElement('img');
    img.src = e.target.src;
    overlay.appendChild(img);
    document.body.appendChild(overlay);
    overlay.addEventListener('click', function() {
      overlay.remove();
    });
  }
});
</script>

<style>
body {
  font-size: 1rem !important;
}

.paper-entry img {
  cursor: zoom-in;
  transition: transform 0.3s ease;
}

.img-overlay {
  position: fixed;
  top: 0; left: 0; right: 0; bottom: 0;
  background: rgba(0,0,0,0.8);
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  z-index: 9999;
  cursor: zoom-out;
}

.img-overlay img {
  max-width: 90vw;
  max-height: 90vh;
  border-radius: 6px;
  box-shadow: 0 0 30px rgba(0,0,0,0.5);
}

.paper-entry {
  display: flex;
  gap: 1rem;
  margin-bottom: 2.2rem;
  align-items: flex-start;
}
.paper-entry img {
  width: 160px;
  flex-shrink: 0;
  border: 1px solid #ddd;
  border-radius: 4px;
}
.paper-info {
  line-height: 1.55;
}
.paper-info .paper-title {
  font-weight: bold;
}
.paper-info .paper-cite {
  color: #555;
  margin: 0.15rem 0 0.4rem 0;
}
.paper-info .paper-summary {
  margin-bottom: 0.3rem;
}
.paper-info .paper-links a {
  margin-right: 0.5rem;
}
@media (max-width: 600px) {
  .paper-entry { flex-direction: column; }
  .paper-entry img { width: 100%; max-width: 280px; }
}
</style>
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| results: asis
#| warning: false
#| message: false

# Publications pipeline:
#   Zotero (collection "_knorre_publications") --Better BibTeX auto-export--> knorre_publications.bib
#   papers.yml holds what the bib cannot: display order, summaries, replication links.
#   _render_papers.R joins the two and emits the paper cards.
# To add a paper: add it in Zotero, pin its citation key, add a papers.yml entry,
# drop images/{key}.png and papers/{key}.pdf, then quarto render.
source("_render_papers.R")
```

## Data

I prepared and maintain the following datasets:

Replication package for ``Unequal by the gun: Four Decades of Black-White Firearm Homicide Gap'' (2026) on [Github](https://github.com/alexknorre/racial_gap_gun_homicides)

Replication package for ''Measuring Changes in Bias-Motivated Attacks: Evidence from Anti-Asian Violence During the COVID-19 Pandemic'' (2025) on [OSF](https://osf.io/skv69/)

Replication package for ''Comparing risks of firearm-related death and injury among young adult males in US cities to wartime service in Iraq and Afghanistan'' (2022) on [Github](https://github.com/alexknorre/safer_at_war)

Russian Crime Victimization Survey, Wave I (2018) on [Dataverse](https://doi.org/10.7910/DVN/C2OTH9), Wave II (2021) on [Dataverse](https://doi.org/10.7910/DVN/SGRQTI)

Homicide rates in Russian cities (2020), data and guide in Russian on [Github](https://github.com/irlcode/russian_cities_homicide_data)

Also, check out [the TV Shows app](https://www.tvshowanalyzer.com).
